title: Re-Pluris project: Identification of multimorbid patients with a higher probability of readmission and mortality
author: susana garcia-gutierrez, susana
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The RePluris project emerges in an increasingly complex healthcare context, where the ageing population and the increase of patients with multiple chronic conditions represent a significant challenge for healthcare systems. The research focuses on a critical problem: the hospital readmission of patients with multimorbidity, a phenomenon that not only impacts on patients' quality of life, but also represents a major burden for the healthcare system. Social determinants play a key role in the likelihood of readmission and mortality of patients with multiple chronic conditions. This innovative project aims to develop predictive models that go beyond traditional clinical indicators, incorporating variables related to the patient's environment and their own perceptions of health. The multi-centre study, carried out in five hospitals in three Spanish regions (Andalusia, Catalonia and the Basque Country), adopts a unique methodological approach. It combines an initial qualitative phase, including focus groups with patients, caregivers and health professionals, with a quantitative phase of prospective follow-up. The main objective is to create stratification tools that allow the personalised identification of patients at higher risk of readmission, facilitating more precise interventions focused on the specific needs of each individual. 
 The expected results will not only contribute to scientific knowledge, but will also provide health systems with practical tools to improve the care of patients with multimorbidity, with the ultimate aim of reducing readmissions and improving their quality of life.
